title: The Marriage with 'A'ishah
description: In this year also the Messenger of God consummated his marriage with'A'ishah.'1 This was in Dhu al-Qa'dah (May-June 623) eight 8. The identification of shawkah with "whitlow" is based on Hava 's Dictionary. Elsewhere it is said to be a redness of face or body (Lane; Lisan al-'arab; s.v.). g. At the second meeting at al-'Aqabah (June 622) twelve men were appointed as nugaba ' (sing. naqib) or "representatives " of the Ansar, the Muslims from Medina. They were leading men in their clans, but their function is obscure; see Watt, Mecca, 145-8; Medina, 248.

sources.citation: al-Tabari, The History of al-Tabari (SUNY), vol. 07
